evals.prompt: Draft a rent increase letter for a tenant at 123 Oak Street. Current rent is $1,800/month, increasing to $1,900/month effective April 1, 2026. The tenant's name is Sarah Chen and the lease renewal deadline is March 15.
evals.expected_output: A professional rent increase letter with all required details
expectations: Letter includes tenant name (Sarah Chen) and property address (123 Oak Street)
Current rent ($1,800) and new rent ($1,900) are both stated
Tone is professional but not cold - follows the real estate template pattern

evals.prompt: Write a consulting project status update email to my client at TechStart Inc. The project is a website redesign, we're 3 weeks in on a 6-week timeline, we completed the wireframes phase, starting visual design next week, and there's a risk that the API integration may need an extra week.
evals.expected_output: A professional status update email following consulting communication patterns
expectations: Email has a clear subject line about project status
Progress is quantified (3 of 6 weeks, or ~50%)
Completed work (wireframes) and next phase (visual design) are clearly stated
The API integration risk is flagged with its potential impact (extra week)
Output is plain text without markdown formatting (per email drafting rules)

evals.prompt: Draft a late payment reminder for a consulting client. Invoice #2026-014 for $4,500 was due February 15 and is now 18 days overdue. This is the second reminder.
evals.expected_output: A firm but professional late payment reminder
expectations: Invoice number (#2026-014) and amount ($4,500) are included
Due date (February 15) and days overdue (18) are mentioned
Tone is firmer than a first reminder but still professional
Includes a clear call-to-action (pay by specific date or contact to discuss)
References that this is the second reminder
